---
title: "Chapman's Goldenrod (Solidago chapmanii)"
slug: "solidago-chapmanii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621795611/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Chapman's Goldenrod"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621795611/medium.jpg" alt="Chapman's Goldenrod" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264490296">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1404306)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry to mesic sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wildflower characterized by its upright stems and clusters of small yellow flower heads arranged along arching branches. In Florida, it is found in dry to mesic sandy habitats such as scrub, sandhills, and open pine woodlands. It typically blooms in fall and provides an important late-season nectar source for a variety of pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1404306-Solidago-chapmanii)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-solidago-odora-chapmanii/)
